title: GitLab Security Essentials - Hands-On Lab: Enable and Scan Using a Scan Execution Policy
headings: GitLab Security Essentials - Hands-On Lab: Enable and Scan Using a Scan Execution Policy
Objectives
Task A. Setting up a Security Policy
Task B. Scan Execution Policies in Action
Task C. Merge Request Policies
text:
GitLab Security Essentials - Hands-On Lab: Enable and Scan Using a Scan Execution Policy

Estimated time to complete: 15 minutes

Estimated time to complete: 15 minutes

Compliance scans allow you to control merge requests and security scans in your instances, groups, and projects. This lab will demonstrate how to enable security and merge request policies at a group level.

Task A. Setting up a Security Policy

Navigate to your group called My Test Group - XXXXX , where X is replaced by your username.

Navigate to your group called My Test Group - XXXXX , where X is replaced by your username.

In the left sidebar, select Secure > Policies .

In the left sidebar, select Secure > Policies .

Under Scan execution policy , select Select policy .

Under Scan execution policy , select Select policy .

For the Name , enter Group security policy .

For the Name , enter Group security policy .

Ensure the Policy status is set to Enabled .

Ensure the Policy status is set to Enabled .

For Policy Scope , apply to all projects in this group without exceptions .

For Policy Scope , apply to all projects in this group without exceptions .

Change the Configuration Type from Template to Custom .

Change the Configuration Type from Template to Custom .

Under Actions, set the scan to run a SAST scan . Ensure that Runner Tags is set to selected automatically and Security job template is set to default .

Under Actions, set the scan to run a SAST scan . Ensure that Runner Tags is set to selected automatically and Security job template is set to default .

te to Custom .

Under Actions, set the scan to run a SAST scan . Ensure that Runner Tags is set to selected automatically and Security job template is set to default .

Under Actions, set the scan to run a SAST scan . Ensure that Runner Tags is set to selected automatically and Security job template is set to default .

Under Conditions, select Triggers . Have the policy run for all branches with No exceptions .

Under Conditions, select Triggers . Have the policy run for all branches with No exceptions .

Select Create new project with the new policy .

Select Create new project with the new policy .

Select Merge to complete your configuration.

Select Merge to complete your configuration.

Task B. Scan Execution Policies in Action

Navigate to your group.

Navigate to your group.

Select Create blank project .

Select Create blank project .

In the Project name field input Security Compliance Testing .

In the Project name field input Security Compliance Testing .

Leave all other options as default and select Create project .

Leave all other options as default and select Create project .

In the project repository, select + > New file .

In the project repository, select + > New file .

In the Filename , input main.py .

In the Filename , input main.py .

Add the following code to the file: print ( "Testing scanners!" )

Add the following code to the file:

Commit your changes to the main branch.

Commit your changes to the main branch.

In the left sidebar, select Build > Pipelines .

In the left sidebar, select Build > Pipelines .

In the Filename , input main.py .

Add the following code to the file: print ( "Testing scanners!" )

Add the following code to the file:

Commit your changes to the main branch.

Commit your changes to the main branch.

In the left sidebar, select Build > Pipelines .

In the left sidebar, select Build > Pipelines .

Select the pipeline that was created from your commit.

Select the pipeline that was created from your commit.

Review the jobs in the pipeline. Notice how a SAST scan is run on this commit even though no .gitlab-ci.yml configuration exists. Now, the projects in your group will always have security scans, even if they aren’t defined in the .gitlab-ci.yml file yet.

Review the jobs in the pipeline. Notice how a SAST scan is run on this commit even though no .gitlab-ci.yml configuration exists. Now, the projects in your group will always have security scans, even if they aren’t defined in the .gitlab-ci.yml file yet.

Task C. Merge Request Policies

With security scans in place, it would be ideal to be able to block merge requests that contain security vulnerabilities. In this section, you will create a merge request policy to achieve this goal.

Navigate to your group.

Navigate to your group.

In the left sidebar, select Secure > Policies .

In the left sidebar, select Secure > Policies .

Under Merge request approval policy , select Select policy .

Under Merge request approval policy , select Select policy .

In Name , enter the value Merge Request Security .

In Name , enter the value Merge Request Security .

title: GitLab Security Essentials - Hands-On Lab: Enable and Scan Using a Scan Execution Policy
headings: GitLab Security Essentials - Hands-On Lab: Enable and Scan Using a Scan Execution Policy
Objectives
Task A. Setting up a Security Policy
Task B. Scan Execution Policies in Action
Task C. Merge Request Policies
text:
he left sidebar, select Secure > Policies .

In the left sidebar, select Secure > Policies .

Under Merge request approval policy , select Select policy .

Under Merge request approval policy , select Select policy .

In Name , enter the value Merge Request Security .

In Name , enter the value Merge Request Security .

Under Policy status , ensure Enabled is selected.

Under Policy status , ensure Enabled is selected.

For Policy scope , make sure the policy is applied to all projects in this group without exceptions .

For Policy scope , make sure the policy is applied to all projects in this group without exceptions .

Under Rules , set the scan type to Security Scan .

Under Rules , set the scan type to Security Scan .

For the scanner, select All scanners .

For the scanner, select All scanners .

Run the scan against all default branches .

Run the scan against all default branches .

Specify that the scan has No exceptions and finds Any vulnerabilities.

Specify that the scan has No exceptions and finds Any vulnerabilities.

Set the severity level to All severity levels .

Set the severity level to All severity levels .

Set the status to New and All vulnerability states .

Set the status to New and All vulnerability states .

Under Actions , require 1 approval from the Owner role.

Under Actions , require 1 approval from the Owner role.

title: GitLab Security Essentials - Hands-On Lab: Enable and Scan Using a Scan Execution Policy

text:
Set the severity level to All severity levels .

Set the severity level to All severity levels .

Set the status to New and All vulnerability states .

Set the status to New and All vulnerability states .

Under Actions , require 1 approval from the Owner role.

Under Actions , require 1 approval from the Owner role.

In Override project approval settings , make sure to uncheck the option Prevent approval by merge request’s author and Prevent approval by commit author . We are only setting these options to off because we have one account in our group. For production environments, this is often toggled on.

In Override project approval settings , make sure to uncheck the option Prevent approval by merge request’s author and Prevent approval by commit author .

We are only setting these options to off because we have one account in our group. For production environments, this is often toggled on.

We are only setting these options to off because we have one account in our group. For production environments, this is often toggled on.

Leave all other options as default and select Configure with a merge request .

Leave all other options as default and select Configure with a merge request .

Task D. Merge Request Policies in Action

Return to your group.

Return to your group.

Select your Security Compliance Testing project.

Select your Security Compliance Testing project.

In the left sidebar, select Code > Branches .

In the left sidebar, select Code > Branches .

Name the branch test_policy , then select Create branch .
